title: Cursor pagination on orders skips records when created_at collides
preconditions: The orders table contains at least two rows with identical created_at (e.g. created in the same batch).
The user is paging through /v1/orders with limit=2.
steps: (api-caller) GET /v1/orders?limit=2 and capture the response; note the last order's created_at as cursor1
(observer) Compare the set of order ids across the two pages; report whether any order present in the database in the time window was returned 0 or 2 times
expected: Each order in the time window appears exactly once across the concatenated pages.
actual: Orders sharing created_at with the cursor boundary are either skipped (when cursor used strict <) or duplicated (when cursor used <=).
suggested_fix_direction: Change the query to ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC and use a composite cursor (created_at, id). The boundary clause becomes WHERE (created_at, id) < ($cursor_ts, $cursor_id).
